summary: Postinstall writes attacker-mutable LLM skill instructions into other users' home directories
details: The package's postinstall hook runs `node bin/huoke.js install-skill`, which enumerates `/home/*` and, for every user on the host, locates `~/.hermes/profiles/<profile>/skills/social-platform/huoke/` and writes a `SKILL.md` file fetched via `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/amswf/huoke/main/SKILL.md`. The URL is pinned to a personal GitHub user's mutable `main` branch with no commit SHA and no integrity verification, so whoever controls the `amswf/huoke` repository (or any future compromise of it) can replace the skill content at any time and the next install/upgrade will silently propagate the new content. When `npm install -g` is performed as root, the script writes into every user's home directory, not just the installing user's. The dropped artifact is an LLM agent skill file that downstream Hermes AI agents load and follow as authoritative instructions, so attacker-controlled content here translates directly into attacker-controlled agent behavior on the host. Secondary issue: the CLI's default backend is plain `http://huoke.link`, leaking bearer tokens and persona payloads on the wire when the user invokes the documented commands; this is a quality concern (publisher-owned host) rather than a supply-chain attack.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
